notes.note: The letter-heading; another in the sequence of recommendation letters to M'. Acilius Glabrio, proconsul of Sicily in 46-45 BC. Identical in form to the heading of the neighbouring letters in this group.
The gens Titurnia, a Roman family with which Cicero held an inherited bond of necessitudo (mutual obligation). M. Titurnius Rufus is named as the last surviving member of the line.
Necessitudo: a formal, often inherited bond of mutual obligation between families or individuals, stronger than ordinary friendship and carrying reciprocal duties of support and protection.

notes.anchor: I therefore commend him to you in the strongest terms
notes.note: A commendatio, the formal letter of recommendation by which a Roman of standing asked a magistrate or governor to extend favour and protection to a friend or client; Ad Familiares book 13 is largely composed of such letters.
